source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 7 | score=1] Celts cross Alps into Italy. 270 BC Celts settle in Asia Minor; Galatia founded c. 400 BC 400 BC 350 BC 300 BC 390 BC Gauls sack Rome. 279 BC Celts invade Greece through Macedonia, temple 125 BC 218 BC Hannibal invades Italy with Celtic mercenaries. forces relieve the city. 250 BC 200BC 150 BC 154 BC Celts raid Massalia; Roman forces relieve the city. of Delphi plundered. however. The Celts of the Danube traded with Greek merchants for centuries, and the Greeks even founded a trading outpost at Massalia (mod- ern Marseilles) with the specific purpose of trading with the Celts of Gaul. Rome and the Celts The Etruscans traded with the Celtic world, but the main period of Celtic expansion came 
[PDF_PAGE 8 | score=1] c. 60 BC 113 BC oman armies defeat Germanic Teutones invade Aedui appeal to Rome for 71BC Cimbri at Noreia. help against Teutones; Rome refuses. 52BC the territory of the Celtic Aedui. 101 BC Cimbri and Teutones defeated at Vercellae. 56 BC Romans defeat Veneti at sea. 100 BC 50 BC AD 102 BC imbri and Teutones defeated at Aix-en-Provence. 82 BC Romans defeat Celts in aly; Roman province of ‘allia Cisalpina formed. expeditions to Britain. 58 BC Helvetii try to leave Switzerland and move into southern Gaul; start of 1st century Possible date of Tain Bo Cualnge. Gallic Wars. who in their turn displaced others, and so on until Rome or one of its Italian allies was threatened. In 58 B.C., when th
